evidence_quotes.quote: I believe the Digital Fairness Act must also apply to video games.
Some games are taken away completely, even if people paid for them.

paragraphs.text: I have never felt the need to write something in public, but I believe the Digital Fairness Act must also apply to video games. This is my feedback: video games need to be protected, and companies should not be allowed to ruin them. The Digital Fairness Act is meant to create a fairer digital space for everyone. However, it often ignores video games, even though they are one of the most important and widely used forms of digital media today. Video games are not just for fun. They are a form of art, storytelling, and emotional connection. Millions of people around the world play them, and they deserve the same protection as other digital products. In recent years, many players have seen their favorite games removed from stores, changed after release, or locked behind unfair online requirements. Some games are taken away completely, even if people paid for them. These actions are not fair to the players. People spend time, money, and passion on these games. They should not lose access just because a company decides to make changes or shut things down. Video games should be respected in the same way as music, movies, or books. Players should be allowed to keep what they paid for. The law should make sure that companies cannot take away or heavily change digital games after they are sold. There should be more transparency, more rights for consumers, and stronger rules to prevent abuse. In conclusion, I believe video games must be included in the Digital Fairness Act. We need to protect this unique and creative part of our culture. Companies should not be allowed to destroy or limit games unfairly. If we want a fair digital world, we cannot leave video games behind.
